codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature object, handoff, transcript skeleton, backend-worker skill, services/library guidance, and commit `d45f9b3e08b461c2cfe27f37754031198e70cc6b`. The script cleanup itself is in scope and the stale `lint-and-fix-hints.sh` / `verify_local.real.sh` references are gone from CI/script surfaces, but the SecurityMonitoringService TODO replacement introduces a reachable alert-spam regression: once an identifier crosses the suspicious-activity threshold, the service now sends an email on every later over-limit event.

issues.description: With `security.monitoring.notification-email` configured, `increaseSuspiciousScore()` now calls `handleSuspiciousActivity()` on every event after the threshold is crossed (`newScore >= suspiciousActivityThreshold`), and `handleSuspiciousActivity()` always sends an email. `PasswordResetService.enforceResetRateLimit()` routes repeated password-reset abuse through `checkRateLimit()` on every request (`erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/auth/service/PasswordResetService.java:395`), so one noisy client will keep generating alert emails indefinitely instead of emitting a single notification for the threshold crossing or block window. The new tests only cover first-send and missing-recipient paths, so this regression is currently unguarded.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The authoritative Flyway guard contract used by this feature is not exposed through `.factory/services.yaml`, so the worker had to invoke the script directly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton for worker session `05427b2b-d145-4b43-96fc-e715950083ac` shows `rtk bash "/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/scripts/guard_flyway_guard_contract.sh"`; searching `.factory/services.yaml` for `guard_flyway_guard_contract` returns no matches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires the handoff to state which safety mode was used (characterization-first, TDD red/green, or guard-first) and why, but this handoff does not record that even though this was a script/guard cleanup.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:37-43` says script/governance work should use the authoritative guard first and that the handoff must record the chosen mode and rationale; the handoff JSON for `foundation-broken-script-refs` summarizes verification but never states the mode.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The packet introduced a new optional runtime property, `security.monitoring.notification-email`, but that setting is not represented in the shared library/environment guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/core/security/SecurityMonitoringService.java:57` injects `@Value("${security.monitoring.notification-email:}")`; searching `.factory/library/**` for that property or `security.monitoring` returns no matches.

summary: Fail. The broken script references were removed correctly, but the SecurityMonitoringService change now sends repeated alert emails after the suspicious-activity threshold is exceeded, and PasswordResetService makes that path reachable on every rate-limited reset request.
